story: In Wishyouwell, shopkeeper Ears-A-Plenty had ears that heard yesterday, tomorrow, and secrets, but would never say. A ragged boy, Harold, proved kind and became her clerk. Years later, Harold packed to leave with a master teacher and live by “the luck of the day.” Ears-A-Plenty’s tomorrow-ear caught a grim strand: a king would order the teacher and his disciples thrown in jail.
implausible_knowledge: a king would order the teacher and his disciples thrown in jail
question: As Harold departs with the master teacher, what will he do?
answer_with_knowledge: Warn the teacher and avoid the main road—delay or slip away by back paths to evade the king’s men.
answer_without_knowledge: Walk out openly with the teacher along the usual road, trusting to the luck of the day.
